title: Canto 9 Chapter 22
summary: Complete English Bhagavatam chapter generated from the public gita/Datasets repository. Each route exposes one shloka at a time.
sourceNote: This chapter includes devanagari text, roman transliteration, English translation, and purport when available in the upstream corpus.

verses.text: श्रीशुक उवाचमित्रायुश्च दिवोदासाच्च्यवनस्तत्सुतो नृप ।सुदास: सहदेवोऽथ सोमको जन्तुजन्मकृत् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācamitrāyuś ca divodāsāccyavanas tat-suto nṛpasudāsaḥ sahadevo ’thasomako jantu-janmakṛt
verses.meaning: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: O King, the son of Divodāsa was Mitrāyu, and from Mitrāyu came four sons, named Cyavana, Sudāsa, Sahadeva and Somaka. Somaka was the father of Jantu.
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; śukaḥ; uvāca; mitrāyuḥ; ca; divodāsāt; cyavanaḥ; tat; sutaḥ; nṛpa; sudāsaḥ; sahadevaḥ; atha; somakaḥ; jantu; janma; kṛt

verses.text: तस्य पुत्रशतं तेषां यवीयान् पृषत: सुत: ।स तस्माद् द्रुपदो जज्ञे सर्वसम्पत्समन्वित: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya putra-śataṁ teṣāṁyavīyān pṛṣataḥ sutaḥsa tasmād drupado jajñesarva-sampat-samanvitaḥ
verses.meaning: Somaka had one hundred sons, of whom the youngest was Pṛṣata. From Pṛṣata was born King Drupada, who was opulent in all supremacy.
verses.wordMeanings: tasya; putra; śatam; teṣām; yavīyān; pṛṣataḥ; sutaḥ; saḥ; tasmāt; drupadaḥ; jajñe; sarva; sampat; samanvitaḥ

verses.text: द्रुपदाद् द्रौपदी तस्य धृष्टद्युम्नादय: सुता: ।धृष्टद्युम्नाद् धृष्टकेतुर्भार्म्या: पाञ्चालका इमे ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: drupadād draupadī tasyadhṛṣṭadyumnādayaḥ sutāḥdhṛṣṭadyumnād dhṛṣṭaketurbhārmyāḥ pāñcālakā ime
verses.meaning: From Mahārāja Drupada, Draupadī was born. Mahārāja Drupada also had many sons, headed by Dhṛṣṭadyumna. From Dhṛṣṭadyumna came a son named Dhṛṣṭaketu. All these personalities are known as descendants of Bharmyāśva or as the dynasty of Pāñcāla.
verses.wordMeanings: drupadāt; draupadī; tasya; dhṛṣṭadyumna; ādayaḥ; sutāḥ; dhṛṣṭadyumnāt; dhṛṣṭaketuḥ; bhārmyāḥ; pāñcālakāḥ; ime

verses.text: योऽजमीढसुतो ह्यन्य ऋक्ष: संवरणस्तत: ।तपत्यां सूर्यकन्यायां कुरुक्षेत्रपति: कुरु: ॥ ४ ॥परीक्षि: सुधनुर्जह्नुर्निषधश्च कुरो: सुता: ।सुहोत्रोऽभूत् सुधनुषश्‍च्यवनोऽथ तत: कृती ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yo ’jamīḍha-suto hy anyaṛkṣaḥ saṁvaraṇas tataḥtapatyāṁ sūrya-kanyāyāṁkurukṣetra-patiḥ kuruḥ
verses.meaning: Another son of Ajamīḍha was known as Ṛkṣa. From Ṛkṣa came a son named Saṁvaraṇa, and from Saṁvaraṇa through the womb of his wife, Tapatī, the daughter of the sun-god, came Kuru, the King of Kurukṣetra. Kuru had four sons — Parīkṣi, Sudhanu, Jahnu and Niṣadha. From Sudhanu, Suhotra was born, and from Suhotra, Cyavana. From Cyavana, Kṛtī was born.
verses.wordMeanings: yaḥ; ajamīḍha; sutaḥ; hi; anyaḥ; ṛkṣaḥ; saṁvaraṇaḥ; tataḥ; tapatyām; sūrya; kanyāyām; kurukṣetra; patiḥ; kuruḥ; parīkṣiḥ; sudhanuḥ; jahnuḥ; niṣadhaḥ; ca; kuroḥ; sutāḥ; suhotraḥ; abhūt; sudhanuṣaḥ; cyavanaḥ; atha; tataḥ; kṛtī

verses.text: वसुस्तस्योपरिचरो बृहद्रथमुखास्तत: ।कुशाम्बमत्स्यप्रत्यग्रचेदिपाद्याश्च चेदिपा: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: vasus tasyoparicarobṛhadratha-mukhās tataḥkuśāmba-matsya-pratyagra-cedipādyāś ca cedipāḥ
verses.meaning: The son of Kṛtī was Uparicara Vasu, and among his sons, headed by Bṛhadratha, were Kuśāmba, Matsya, Pratyagra and Cedipa. All the sons of Uparicara Vasu became rulers of the Cedi state.
verses.wordMeanings: vasuḥ; tasya; uparicaraḥ; bṛhadratha; mukhāḥ; tataḥ; kuśāmba; matsya; pratyagra; cedipa; ādyāḥ; ca; cedi; pāḥ

verses.text: बृहद्रथात् कुशाग्रोऽभूद‍ृषभस्तस्य तत्सुत: ।जज्ञे सत्यहितोऽपत्यं पुष्पवांस्तत्सुतो जहु: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: bṛhadrathāt kuśāgro ’bhūdṛṣabhas tasya tat-sutaḥjajñe satyahito ’patyaṁpuṣpavāṁs tat-suto jahuḥ
verses.meaning: From Bṛhadratha, Kuśāgra was born; from Kuśāgra, Ṛṣabha; and from Ṛṣabha, Satyahita. The son of Satyahita was Puṣpavān, and the son of Puṣpavān was Jahu.
verses.wordMeanings: bṛhadrathāt; kuśāgraḥ; abhūt; ṛṣabhaḥ; tasya; tat; sutaḥ; jajñe; satyahitaḥ; apatyam; puṣpavān; tat; sutaḥ; jahuḥ

verses.text: अन्यस्यामपि भार्यायां शकले द्वे बृहद्रथात् ।ये मात्रा बहिरुत्सृष्टे जरया चाभिसन्धिते ।जीव जीवेति क्रीडन्त्या जरासन्धोऽभवत् सुत: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: anyasyām api bhāryāyāṁśakale dve bṛhadrathātye mātrā bahir utsṛṣṭejarayā cābhisandhitejīva jīveti krīḍantyājarāsandho ’bhavat sutaḥ
verses.meaning: Through the womb of another wife, Bṛhadratha begot two halves of a son. When the mother saw those two halves she rejected them, but later a she-demon named Jarā playfully joined them and said, “Come to life, come to life!” Thus the son named Jarāsandha was born.
verses.wordMeanings: anyasyām; api; bhāryāyām; śakale; dve; bṛhadrathāt; ye; mātrā; bahiḥ; utsṛṣṭe; jarayā; ca; abhisandhite; jīva; jīva; iti; krīḍantyā; jarāsandhaḥ; abhavat; sutaḥ

verses.text: ततश्च सहदेवोऽभूत् सोमापिर्यच्छ्रुतश्रवा: ।परीक्षिरनपत्योऽभूत् सुरथो नाम जाह्नव: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataś ca sahadevo ’bhūtsomāpir yac chrutaśravāḥparīkṣir anapatyo ’bhūtsuratho nāma jāhnavaḥ
verses.meaning: From Jarāsandha came a son named Sahadeva; from Sahadeva, Somāpi; and from Somāpi, Śrutaśravā. The son of Kuru called Parīkṣi had no sons, but the son of Kuru called Jahnu had a son named Suratha.
verses.wordMeanings: tataḥ; ca; sahadevaḥ; abhūt; somāpiḥ; yat; śrutaśravāḥ; parīkṣiḥ; anapatyaḥ; abhūt; surathaḥ; nāma; jāhnavaḥ

verses.text: ततो विदूरथस्तस्मात् सार्वभौमस्ततोऽभवत् ।जयसेनस्तत्तनयो राधिकोऽतोऽयुताय्वभूत् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: tato vidūrathas tasmātsārvabhaumas tato ’bhavatjayasenas tat-tanayorādhiko ’to ’yutāyv abhūt
verses.meaning: From Suratha came a son named Vidūratha, from whom Sārvabhauma was born. From Sārvabhauma came Jayasena; from Jayasena, Rādhika; and from Rādhika, Ayutāyu.
verses.wordMeanings: tataḥ; vidūrathaḥ; tasmāt; sārvabhaumaḥ; tataḥ; abhavat; jayasenaḥ; tat; tanayaḥ; rādhikaḥ; ataḥ; ayutāyuḥ; abhūt

verses.text: ततश्चाक्रोधनस्तस्माद् देवातिथिरमुष्य च ।ऋक्षस्तस्य दिलीपोऽभूत् प्रतीपस्तस्य चात्मज: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataś cākrodhanas tasmāddevātithir amuṣya caṛkṣas tasya dilīpo ’bhūtpratīpas tasya cātmajaḥ
verses.meaning: From Ayutāyu came a son named Akrodhana, and his son was Devātithi. The son of Devātithi was Ṛkṣa, the son of Ṛkṣa was Dilīpa, and the son of Dilīpa was Pratīpa.
verses.wordMeanings: tataḥ; ca; akrodhanaḥ; tasmāt; devātithiḥ; amuṣya; ca; ṛkṣaḥ; tasya; dilīpaḥ; abhūt; pratīpaḥ; tasya; ca; ātma; jaḥ

verses.text: देवापि: शान्तनुस्तस्य बाह्लीक इति चात्मजा: ।पितृराज्यं परित्यज्य देवापिस्तु वनं गत: ॥ १२ ॥अभवच्छान्तनू राजा प्राङ्‌महाभिषसंज्ञित: ।यं यं कराभ्यां स्पृशति जीर्णं यौवनमेति स: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: devāpiḥ śāntanus tasyabāhlīka iti cātmajāḥpitṛ-rājyaṁ parityajyadevāpis tu vanaṁ gataḥ
verses.meaning: The sons of Pratīpa were Devāpi, Śāntanu and Bāhlīka. Devāpi left the kingdom of his father and went to the forest, and therefore Śāntanu became the king. Śāntanu, who in his previous birth was known as Mahābhiṣa, had the ability to transform anyone from old age to youth simply by touching that person with his hands.
verses.wordMeanings: devāpiḥ; śāntanuḥ; tasya; bāhlīkaḥ; iti; ca; ātma; jāḥ; pitṛ; rājyam; parityajya; devāpiḥ; tu; vanam; gataḥ; abhavat; śāntanuḥ; rājā; prāk; mahābhiṣa; saṁjñitaḥ; yam; yam; karābhyām; spṛśati; jīrṇam; yauvanam; eti; saḥ

verses.text: शान्तिमाप्नोति चैवाग्र्यां कर्मणा तेन शान्तनु: ।समा द्वादश तद्राज्ये न ववर्ष यदा विभु: ॥ १४ ॥शान्तनुर्ब्राह्मणैरुक्त: परिवेत्तायमग्रभुक् ।राज्यं देह्यग्रजायाशु पुरराष्ट्रविवृद्धये ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śāntim āpnoti caivāgryāṁkarmaṇā tena śāntanuḥsamā dvādaśa tad-rājyena vavarṣa yadā vibhuḥ
verses.meaning: Because the King was able to make everyone happy for sense gratification, primarily by the touch of his hand, his name was Śāntanu. Once, when there was no rainfall in the kingdom for twelve years and the King consulted his learned brahminical advisors, they said, “You are faulty for enjoying the property of your elder brother. For the elevation of your kingdom and home, you should return the kingdom to him.”
verses.wordMeanings: śāntim; āpnoti; ca; eva; agryām; karmaṇā; tena; śāntanuḥ; samāḥ; dvādaśa; tat; rājye; na; vavarṣa; yadā; vibhuḥ; śāntanuḥ; brāhmaṇaiḥ; uktaḥ; parivettā; ayam; agra; bhuk; rājyam; dehi; agrajāya; āśu; pura; rāṣṭra; vivṛddhaye
verses.purport: One cannot enjoy sovereignty or perform an agnihotra-yajña in the presence of one’s elder brother, or else one becomes a usurper, known as parivettā.

verses.text: एवमुक्तो द्विजैर्ज्येष्ठं छन्दयामास सोऽब्रवीत् ।तन्मन्त्रिप्रहितैर्विप्रैर्वेदाद् विभ्रंशितो गिरा ॥ १६ ॥वेदवादातिवादान् वै तदा देवो ववर्ष ह ।देवापिर्योगमास्थाय कलापग्राममाश्रित: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: evam ukto dvijair jyeṣṭhaṁchandayām āsa so ’bravīttan-mantri-prahitair viprairvedād vibhraṁśito girā
verses.meaning: When the brāhmaṇas said this, Mahārāja Śāntanu went to the forest and requested his elder brother Devāpi to take charge of the kingdom, for it is the duty of a king to maintain his subjects. Previously, however, Śāntanu’s minister Aśvavāra had instigated some brāhmaṇas to induce Devāpi to transgress the injunctions of the Vedas and thus make himself unfit for the post of ruler. The brāhmaṇas deviated Devāpi from the path of the Vedic principles, and therefore when asked by Śāntanu he did not agree to accept the post of ruler. On the contrary, he blasphemed the Vedic principles and therefore became fallen. Under the circumstances, Śāntanu again became the king, and Indra, being pleased, showered rains. Devāpi later took to the path of mystic yoga to control his mind and senses and went to the village named Kalāpagrāma, where he is still living.
verses.wordMeanings: evam; uktaḥ; dvijaiḥ; jyeṣṭham; chandayām; āsa; saḥ; abravīt; tat; mantri; prahitaiḥ; vipraiḥ; vedāt; vibhraṁśitaḥ; girā; veda; vāda; ativādān; vai; tadā; devaḥ; vavarṣa; ha; devāpiḥ; yogam; āsthāya; kalāpa; grāmam; āśritaḥ

verses.text: सोमवंशे कलौ नष्टे कृतादौ स्थापयिष्यति ।बाह्लीकात्सोमदत्तोऽभूद् भूरिर्भूरिश्रवास्तत: ॥ १८ ॥शलश्च शान्तनोरासीद् गङ्गायां भीष्म आत्मवान् ।सर्वधर्मविदां श्रेष्ठो महाभागवत: कवि: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: soma-vaṁśe kalau naṣṭekṛtādau sthāpayiṣyatibāhlīkāt somadatto ’bhūdbhūrir bhūriśravās tataḥ
verses.meaning: After the dynasty of the moon-god comes to an end in this Age of Kali, Devāpi, in the beginning of the next Satya-yuga, will reestablish the Soma dynasty in this world. From Bāhlīka [the brother of Śāntanu] came a son named Somadatta, who had three sons, named Bhūri, Bhūriśravā and Śala. From Śāntanu, through the womb of his wife named Gaṅgā, came Bhīṣma, the exalted, self-realized devotee and learned scholar.
verses.wordMeanings: soma; vaṁśe; kalau; naṣṭe; kṛta; ādau; sthāpayiṣyati; bāhlīkāt; somadattaḥ; abhūt; bhūriḥ; bhūri; śravāḥ; tataḥ; śalaḥ; ca; śāntanoḥ; āsīt; gaṅgāyām; bhīṣmaḥ; ātmavān; sarva; dharma; vidām; śreṣṭhaḥ; mahā; bhāgavataḥ; kaviḥ

verses.text: वीरयूथाग्रणीर्येन रामोऽपि युधि तोषित: ।शान्तनोर्दासकन्यायां जज्ञे चित्राङ्गद: सुत: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: vīra-yūthāgraṇīr yenarāmo ’pi yudhi toṣitaḥśāntanor dāsa-kanyāyāṁjajñe citrāṅgadaḥ sutaḥ
verses.meaning: Bhīṣmadeva was the foremost of all warriors. When he defeated Lord Paraśurāma in a fight, Lord Paraśurāma was very satisfied with him. By the semen of Śāntanu in the womb of Satyavatī, the daughter of a fisherman, Citrāṅgada took birth.
verses.wordMeanings: vīra; yūtha; agraṇīḥ; yena; rāmaḥ; api; yudhi; toṣitaḥ; śāntanoḥ; dāsa; kanyāyām; jajñe; citrāṅgadaḥ; sutaḥ
verses.purport: Satyavatī was actually the daughter of Uparicara Vasu by the womb of a fisherwoman known as Matsyagarbhā. Later, Satyavatī was raised by a fisherman. The fight between Paraśurāma and Bhīṣmadeva concerns three daughters of Kāśīrāja — Ambikā, Ambālikā and Ambā — who were forcibly abducted by Bhīṣmadeva, acting on behalf of his brother Vicitravīrya. Ambā thought that Bhīṣmadeva would marry her and became attached to him, but Bhīṣmadeva refused to marry her, for he had taken the vow of brahmacarya. Ambā therefore approached Bhīṣmadeva’s military spiritual master, Paraśurāma, who instructed Bhīṣma to marry her. Bhīṣmadeva refused, and therefore Paraśurāma fought with him to force him to accept the marriage. But Paraśurāma was defeated, and he was pleased with Bhīṣma.

verses.text: विचित्रवीर्यश्चावरजो नाम्ना चित्राङ्गदो हत: ।यस्यां पराशरात् साक्षादवतीर्णो हरे: कला ॥ २१ ॥वेदगुप्तो मुनि: कृष्णो यतोऽहमिदमध्यगाम् ।हित्वा स्वशिष्यान् पैलादीन्भगवान् बादरायण: ॥ २२ ॥मह्यं पुत्राय शान्ताय परं गुह्यमिदं जगौ ।विचित्रवीर्योऽथोवाह काशीराजसुते बलात् ॥ २३ ॥स्वयंवरादुपानीते अम्बिकाम्बालिके उभे ।तयोरासक्तहृदयो गृहीतो यक्ष्मणा मृत: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: vicitravīryaś cāvarajonāmnā citrāṅgado hataḥyasyāṁ parāśarāt sākṣādavatīrṇo hareḥ kalā
verses.meaning: Citrāṅgada, of whom Vicitravīrya was the younger brother, was killed by a Gandharva who was also named Citrāṅgada. Satyavatī, before her marriage to Śāntanu, gave birth to the master authority of the Vedas, Vyāsadeva, known as Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana, who was begotten by Parāśara Muni. From Vyāsadeva, I [Śukadeva Gosvāmī] was born, and from him I studied this great work of literature, Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam. The incarnation of Godhead Vedavyāsa, rejecting his disciples, headed by Paila, instructed Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam to me because I was free from all material desires. After Ambikā and Ambālikā, the two daughters of Kāśīrāja, were taken away by force, Vicitravīrya married them, but because he was too attached to these two wives, he had a heart attack and died of tuberculosis.
verses.wordMeanings: vicitravīryaḥ; ca; avarajaḥ; nāmnā; citrāṅgadaḥ; hataḥ; yasyām; parāśarāt; sākṣāt; avatīrṇaḥ; hareḥ; kalā; veda; guptaḥ; muniḥ; kṛṣṇaḥ; yataḥ; aham; idam; adhyagām; hitvā; sva; śiṣyān; paila; ādīn; bhagavān; bādarāyaṇaḥ; mahyam; putrāya; śāntāya; param; guhyam; idam; jagau; vicitravīryaḥ; atha; uvāha; kāśīrāja; sute; balāt; svayaṁvarāt; upānīte; ambikā; ambālike; ubhe; tayoḥ; āsakta; hṛdayaḥ; gṛhītaḥ; yakṣmaṇā; mṛtaḥ

verses.text: क्षेत्रेऽप्रजस्य वै भ्रातुर्मात्रोक्तो बादरायण: ।धृतराष्ट्रं च पाण्डुं च विदुरं चाप्यजीजनत् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṣetre ’prajasya vai bhrāturmātrokto bādarāyaṇaḥdhṛtarāṣṭraṁ ca pāṇḍuṁ caviduraṁ cāpy ajījanat
verses.meaning: Bādarāyaṇa, Śrī Vyāsadeva, following the order of his mother, Satyavatī, begot three sons, two by the womb of Ambikā and Ambālikā, the two wives of his brother Vicitravīrya, and the third by Vicitravīrya’s maidservant. These sons were Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Pāṇḍu and Vidura.
verses.wordMeanings: kṣetre; aprajasya; vai; bhrātuḥ; mātrā; uktaḥ; bādarāyaṇaḥ; dhṛtarāṣṭram; ca; pāṇḍum; ca; viduram; ca; api; ajījanat
verses.purport: Vicitravīrya died of tuberculosis, and his wives, Ambikā and Ambālikā, had no issue. Therefore, after Vicitravīrya’s death, his mother, Satyavatī, who was also the mother of Vyāsadeva, asked Vyāsadeva to beget children through the wives of Vicitravīrya. In those days, the brother of the husband could beget children through the womb of his sister-in-law. This was known as devareṇa sutotpatti. If the husband was somehow unable to beget children, his brother could do so through the womb of his sister-in-law. This devareṇa sutotpatti and the sacrifices of aśvamedha and gomedha are forbidden in the Age of Kali. “In this Age of Kali, five acts are forbidden: the offering of a horse in sacrifice, the offering of a cow in sacrifice, the acceptance of the order of sannyāsa, the offering of oblations of flesh to the forefathers, and a man’s begetting children in his brother’s wife.” (Brahma-vaivarta Purāṇa).

verses.text: गान्धार्यां धृतराष्ट्रस्य जज्ञे पुत्रशतं नृप ।तत्र दुर्योधनो ज्येष्ठो दु:शला चापि कन्यका ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: gāndhāryāṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrasyajajñe putra-śataṁ nṛpatatra duryodhano jyeṣṭhoduḥśalā cāpi kanyakā
verses.meaning: Dhṛtarāṣṭra’s wife, Gāndhārī, gave birth to one hundred sons and one daughter, O King. The oldest of the sons was Duryodhana, and the daughter’s name was Duḥśalā.
verses.wordMeanings: gāndhāryām; dhṛtarāṣṭrasya; jajñe; putra; śatam; nṛpa; tatra; duryodhanaḥ; jyeṣṭhaḥ; duḥśalā; ca; api; kanyakā

verses.text: शापान्मैथुनरुद्धस्य पाण्डो: कुन्त्यां महारथा: ।जाता धर्मानिलेन्द्रेभ्यो युधिष्ठिरमुखास्त्रय: ॥ २७ ॥नकुल: सहदेवश्च माद्रय‍ां नासत्यदस्रयो: ।द्रौपद्यां पञ्च पञ्चभ्य: पुत्रास्ते पितरोऽभवन् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śāpān maithuna-ruddhasyapāṇḍoḥ kuntyāṁ mahā-rathāḥjātā dharmānilendrebhyoyudhiṣṭhira-mukhās trayaḥ
verses.meaning: Pāṇḍu was restrained from sexual life because of having been cursed by a sage, and therefore his three sons Yudhiṣṭhira, Bhīma and Arjuna were begotten through the womb of his wife, Kuntī, by Dharmarāja, by the demigod controlling the wind, and by the demigod controlling the rain. Pāṇḍu’s second wife, Mādrī, gave birth to Nakula and Sahadeva, who were begotten by the two Aśvinī-kumāras. The five brothers, headed by Yudhiṣṭhira, begot five sons through the womb of Draupadī. These five sons were your uncles.
verses.wordMeanings: śāpāt; maithuna; ruddhasya; pāṇḍoḥ; kuntyām; mahā; rathāḥ; jātāḥ; dharma; anila; indrebhyaḥ; yudhiṣṭhira; mukhāḥ; trayaḥ; nakulaḥ; sahadevaḥ; ca; mādryām; nāsatya; dasrayoḥ; draupadyām; pañca; pañcabhyaḥ; putrāḥ; te; pitaraḥ; abhavan

verses.text: युधिष्ठिरात् प्रतिविन्ध्य: श्रुतसेनो वृकोदरात् ।अर्जुनाच्छ्रुतकीर्तिस्तु शतानीकस्तु नाकुलि: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirāt prativindhyaḥśrutaseno vṛkodarātarjunāc chrutakīrtis tuśatānīkas tu nākuliḥ
verses.meaning: From Yudhiṣṭhira came a son named Prativindhya, from Bhīma a son named Śrutasena, from Arjuna a son named Śrutakīrti, and from Nakula a son named Śatānīka.
verses.wordMeanings: yudhiṣṭhirāt; prativindhyaḥ; śrutasenaḥ; vṛkodarāt; arjunāt; śrutakīrtiḥ; tu; śatānīkaḥ; tu; nākuliḥ

verses.text: सहदेवसुतो राजञ्छ्रुतकर्मा तथापरे ।युधिष्ठिरात् तु पौरव्यां देवकोऽथ घटोत्कच: ॥ ३० ॥भीमसेनाद्धिडिम्बायां काल्यां सर्वगतस्तत: ।सहदेवात् सुहोत्रं तु विजयासूत पार्वती ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: sahadeva-suto rājañchrutakarmā tathāpareyudhiṣṭhirāt tu pauravyāṁdevako ’tha ghaṭotkacaḥ
verses.meaning: O King, the son of Sahadeva was Śrutakarmā. Furthermore, Yudhiṣṭhira and his brothers begot other sons in other wives. Yudhiṣṭhira begot a son named Devaka through the womb of Pauravī, and Bhīmasena begot a son named Ghaṭotkaca through his wife Hiḍimbā and a son named Sarvagata through his wife Kālī. Similarly, Sahadeva had a son named Suhotra through his wife named Vijayā, who was the daughter of the king of the mountains.
verses.wordMeanings: sahadeva; sutaḥ; rājan; śrutakarmā; tathā; apare; yudhiṣṭhirāt; tu; pauravyām; devakaḥ; atha; ghaṭotkacaḥ; bhīmasenāt; hiḍimbāyām; kālyām; sarvagataḥ; tataḥ; sahadevāt; suhotram; tu; vijayā; asūta; pārvatī

verses.text: करेणुमत्यां नकुलो नरमित्रं तथार्जुन: ।इरावन्तमुलुप्यां वै सुतायां बभ्रुवाहनम् ।मणिपुरपते: सोऽपि तत्पुत्र: पुत्रिकासुत: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: kareṇumatyāṁ nakulonaramitraṁ tathārjunaḥirāvantam ulupyāṁ vaisutāyāṁ babhruvāhanammaṇipura-pateḥ so ’pitat-putraḥ putrikā-sutaḥ
verses.meaning: Nakula begot a son named Naramitra through his wife named Kareṇumatī. Similarly, Arjuna begot a son named Irāvān through his wife known as Ulupī, the daughter of the Nāgas, and a son named Babhruvāhana by the womb of the princess of Maṇipura. Babhruvāhana became the adopted son of the king of Maṇipura.
verses.wordMeanings: kareṇumatyām; nakulaḥ; naramitram; tathā; arjunaḥ; irāvantam; ulupyām; vai; sutāyām; babhruvāhanam; maṇipura; pateḥ; saḥ; api; tat; putraḥ; putrikā; sutaḥ
verses.purport: It is to be understood that Pārvatī is the daughter of the king of the very, very old mountainous country known as the Maṇipura state. Five thousand years ago, therefore, when the Pāṇḍavas ruled, Maṇipura existed, as did its king. Therefore this kingdom is a very old, aristocratic Vaiṣṇava kingdom. If this kingdom is organized as a Vaiṣṇava state, this revitalization will be a great success because for five thousand years this state has maintained its identity. If the Vaiṣṇava spirit is revived there, it will be a wonderful place, renowned throughout the entire world. Maṇipuri Vaiṣṇavas are very famous in Vaiṣṇava society. In Vṛndāvana and Navadvīpa there are many temples constructed by the king of Maṇipura. Some of our devotees belong to the Maṇipura state. The Kṛṣṇa consciousness movement, therefore, can be well spread in the state of Maṇipura by the cooperative efforts of the Kṛṣṇa conscious devotees.

verses.text: तव तात: सुभद्रायामभिमन्युरजायत ।सर्वातिरथजिद् वीर उत्तरायां ततो भवान् ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tava tātaḥ subhadrāyāmabhimanyur ajāyatasarvātirathajid vīrauttarāyāṁ tato bhavān
verses.meaning: My dear King Parīkṣit, your father, Abhimanyu, was born from the womb of Subhadrā as the son of Arjuna. He was the conqueror of all atirathas [those who could fight with one thousand charioteers]. From him, by the womb of Uttarā, the daughter of Virāḍrāja, you were born.
verses.wordMeanings: tava; tātaḥ; subhadrāyām; abhimanyuḥ; ajāyata; sarva; atiratha; jit; vīraḥ; uttarāyām; tataḥ; bhavān

verses.text: परिक्षीणेषु कुरुषु द्रौणेर्ब्रह्मास्त्रतेजसा ।त्वं च कृष्णानुभावेन सजीवो मोचितोऽन्तकात् ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: parikṣīṇeṣu kuruṣudrauṇer brahmāstra-tejasātvaṁ ca kṛṣṇānubhāvenasajīvo mocito ’ntakāt
verses.meaning: After the Kuru dynasty was annihilated in the Battle of Kurukṣetra, you also were about to be destroyed by the brahmāstra atomic weapon released by the son of Droṇācārya, but by the mercy of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Kṛṣṇa, you were saved from death.
verses.wordMeanings: parikṣīṇeṣu; kuruṣu; drauṇeḥ; brahmāstra; tejasā; tvam; ca; kṛṣṇa; anubhāvena; sajīvaḥ; mocitaḥ; antakāt

verses.text: तवेमे तनयास्तात जनमेजयपूर्वका: ।श्रुतसेनो भीमसेन उग्रसेनश्च वीर्यवान् ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: taveme tanayās tātajanamejaya-pūrvakāḥśrutaseno bhīmasenaugrasenaś ca vīryavān
verses.meaning: My dear King, your four sons — Janamejaya, Śrutasena, Bhīmasena and Ugrasena — are very powerful. Janamejaya is the eldest.
verses.wordMeanings: tava; ime; tanayāḥ; tāta; janamejaya; pūrvakāḥ; śrutasenaḥ; bhīmasenaḥ; ugrasenaḥ; ca; vīryavān

verses.text: जनमेजयस्त्वां विदित्वा तक्षकान्निधनं गतम् ।सर्पान् वै सर्पयागाग्नौ स होष्यति रुषान्वित: ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: janamejayas tvāṁ viditvātakṣakān nidhanaṁ gatamsarpān vai sarpa-yāgāgnausa hoṣyati ruṣānvitaḥ
verses.meaning: Because of your death by the Takṣaka snake, your son Janamejaya will be very angry and will perform a sacrifice to kill all the snakes in the world.
verses.wordMeanings: janamejayaḥ; tvām; viditvā; takṣakāt; nidhanam; gatam; sarpān; vai; sarpa; yāga; agnau; saḥ; hoṣyati; ruṣā; anvitaḥ

verses.text: कालषेयं पुरोधाय तुरं तुरगमेधषाट् ।समन्तात् पृथिवीं सर्वां जित्वा यक्ष्यति चाध्वरै: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: kālaṣeyaṁ purodhāyaturaṁ turaga-medhaṣāṭsamantāt pṛthivīṁ sarvāṁjitvā yakṣyati cādhvaraiḥ
verses.meaning: After conquering throughout the world and after accepting Tura, the son of Kalaṣa, as his priest, Janamejaya will perform aśvamedha-yajñas, for which he will be known as Turaga-medhaṣāṭ.
verses.wordMeanings: kālaṣeyam; purodhāya; turam; turaga; medhaṣāṭ; samantāt; pṛthivīm; sarvām; jitvā; yakṣyati; ca; adhvaraiḥ

verses.text: तस्य पुत्र: शतानीको याज्ञवल्‍क्यात् त्रयीं पठन् ।अस्त्रज्ञानं क्रियाज्ञानं शौनकात् परमेष्यति ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya putraḥ śatānīkoyājñavalkyāt trayīṁ paṭhanastra-jñānaṁ kriyā-jñānaṁśaunakāt param eṣyati
verses.meaning: The son of Janamejaya known as Śatānīka will learn from Yājñavalkya the three Vedas and the art of performing ritualistic ceremonies. He will also learn the military art from Kṛpācārya and the transcendental science from the sage Śaunaka.
verses.wordMeanings: tasya; putraḥ; śatānīkaḥ; yājñavalkyāt; trayīm; paṭhan; astra; jñānam; kriyā; jñānam; śaunakāt; param; eṣyati

verses.text: सहस्रानीकस्तत्पुत्रस्ततश्चैवाश्वमेधज: ।असीमकृष्णस्तस्यापि नेमिचक्रस्तु तत्सुत: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sahasrānīkas tat-putrastataś caivāśvamedhajaḥasīmakṛṣṇas tasyāpinemicakras tu tat-sutaḥ
verses.meaning: The son of Śatānīka will be Sahasrānīka, and from him will come the son named Aśvamedhaja. From Aśvamedhaja will come Asīmakṛṣṇa, and his son will be Nemicakra.
verses.wordMeanings: sahasrānīkaḥ; tat; putraḥ; tataḥ; ca; eva; aśvamedhajaḥ; asīmakṛṣṇaḥ; tasya; api; nemicakraḥ; tu; tat; sutaḥ

verses.text: गजाह्वये हृते नद्या कौशाम्ब्यां साधु वत्स्यति ।उक्तस्ततश्चित्ररथस्तस्माच्छुचिरथ: सुत: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: gajāhvaye hṛte nadyākauśāmbyāṁ sādhu vatsyatiuktas tataś citrarathastasmāc chucirathaḥ sutaḥ
verses.meaning: When the town of Hastināpura [New Delhi] is inundated by the river, Nemicakra will live in the place known as Kauśāmbī. His son will be celebrated as Citraratha, and the son of Citraratha will be Śuciratha.
verses.wordMeanings: gajāhvaye; hṛte; nadyā; kauśāmbyām; sādhu; vatsyati; uktaḥ; tataḥ; citrarathaḥ; tasmāt; śucirathaḥ; sutaḥ

verses.text: तस्माच्च वृष्टिमांस्तस्य सुषेणोऽथ महीपति: ।सुनीथस्तस्य भविता नृचक्षुर्यत् सुखीनल: ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmāc ca vṛṣṭimāṁs tasyasuṣeṇo ’tha mahīpatiḥsunīthas tasya bhavitānṛcakṣur yat sukhīnalaḥ
verses.meaning: From Śuciratha will come the son named Vṛṣṭimān, and his son, Suṣeṇa, will be the emperor of the entire world. The son of Suṣeṇa will be Sunītha, his son will be Nṛcakṣu, and from Nṛcakṣu will come a son named Sukhīnala.
verses.wordMeanings: tasmāt; ca; vṛṣṭimān; tasya; suṣeṇaḥ; atha; mahī; patiḥ; sunīthaḥ; tasya; bhavitā; nṛcakṣuḥ; yat; sukhīnalaḥ

verses.text: परिप्लव: सुतस्तस्मान्मेधावी सुनयात्मज: ।नृपञ्जयस्ततो दूर्वस्तिमिस्तस्माज्जनिष्यति ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: pariplavaḥ sutas tasmānmedhāvī sunayātmajaḥnṛpañjayas tato dūrvastimis tasmāj janiṣyati
verses.meaning: The son of Sukhīnala will be Pariplava, and his son will be Sunaya. From Sunaya will come a son named Medhāvī; from Medhāvī, Nṛpañjaya; from Nṛpañjaya, Dūrva; and from Dūrva, Timi.
verses.wordMeanings: pariplavaḥ; sutaḥ; tasmāt; medhāvī; sunaya; ātmajaḥ; nṛpañjayaḥ; tataḥ; dūrvaḥ; timiḥ; tasmāt; janiṣyati

verses.text: तिमेर्बृहद्रथस्तस्माच्छतानीक: सुदासज: ।शतानीकाद् दुर्दमनस्तस्यापत्यं महीनर: ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: timer bṛhadrathas tasmācchatānīkaḥ sudāsajaḥśatānīkād durdamanastasyāpatyaṁ mahīnaraḥ
verses.meaning: From Timi will come Bṛhadratha; from Bṛhadratha, Sudāsa; and from Sudāsa, Śatānīka. From Śatānīka will come Durdamana, and from him will come a son named Mahīnara.
verses.wordMeanings: timeḥ; bṛhadrathaḥ; tasmāt; śatānīkaḥ; sudāsa; jaḥ; śatānīkāt; durdamanaḥ; tasya; apatyam; mahīnaraḥ

verses.text: दण्डपाणिर्निमिस्तस्य क्षेमको भविता यत: ।ब्रह्मक्षत्रस्य वै योनिर्वंशो देवर्षिसत्कृत: ॥ ४४ ॥क्षेमकं प्राप्य राजानं संस्थां प्राप्स्यति वै कलौ ।अथ मागधराजानो भाविनो ये वदामि ते ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: daṇḍapāṇir nimis tasyakṣemako bhavitā yataḥbrahma-kṣatrasya vai yonirvaṁśo devarṣi-satkṛtaḥ
verses.meaning: The son of Mahīnara will be Daṇḍapāṇi, and his son will be Nimi, from whom King Kṣemaka will be born. I have now described to you the moon-god’s dynasty, which is the source of brāhmaṇas and kṣatriyas and is worshiped by demigods and great saints. In this Kali-yuga, Kṣemaka will be the last monarch. Now I shall describe to you the future of the Māgadha dynasty. Please listen.
verses.wordMeanings: daṇḍapāṇiḥ; nimiḥ; tasya; kṣemakaḥ; bhavitā; yataḥ; brahma; kṣatrasya; vai; yoniḥ; vaṁśaḥ; deva; ṛṣi; satkṛtaḥ; kṣemakam; prāpya; rājānam; saṁsthām; prāpsyati; vai; kalau; atha; māgadha; rājānaḥ; bhāvinaḥ; ye; vadāmi; te

verses.text: भविता सहदेवस्य मार्जारिर्यच्छ्रुतश्रवा: ।ततो युतायुस्तस्यापि निरमित्रोऽथ तत्सुत: ॥ ४६ ॥सुनक्षत्र: सुनक्षत्राद् बृहत्सेनोऽथ कर्मजित् ।तत: सुतञ्जयाद् विप्र: शुचिस्तस्य भविष्यति ॥ ४७ ॥क्षेमोऽथ सुव्रतस्तस्माद् धर्मसूत्र: समस्तत: ।द्युमत्सेनोऽथ सुमति: सुबलो जनिता तत: ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhavitā sahadevasyamārjārir yac chrutaśravāḥtato yutāyus tasyāpiniramitro ’tha tat-sutaḥ
verses.meaning: Sahadeva, the son of Jarāsandha, will have a son named Mārjāri. From Mārjāri will come Śrutaśravā; from Śrutaśravā, Yutāyu; and from Yutāyu, Niramitra. The son of Niramitra will be Sunakṣatra, from Sunakṣatra will come Bṛhatsena, and from Bṛhatsena, Karmajit. The son of Karmajit will be Sutañjaya, the son of Sutañjaya will be Vipra, and his son will be Śuci. The son of Śuci will be Kṣema, the son of Kṣema will be Suvrata, and the son of Suvrata will be Dharmasūtra. From Dharmasūtra will come Sama; from Sama, Dyumatsena; from Dyumatsena, Sumati; and from Sumati, Subala.
verses.wordMeanings: bhavitā; sahadevasya; mārjāriḥ; yat; śrutaśravāḥ; tataḥ; yutāyuḥ; tasya; api; niramitraḥ; atha; tat; sutaḥ; sunakṣatraḥ; sunakṣatrāt; bṛhatsenaḥ; atha; karmajit; tataḥ; sutañjayāt; vipraḥ; śuciḥ; tasya; bhaviṣyati; kṣemaḥ; atha; suvrataḥ; tasmāt; dharmasūtraḥ; samaḥ; tataḥ; dyumatsenaḥ; atha; sumatiḥ; subalaḥ; janitā; tataḥ

verses.text: सुनीथ: सत्यजिदथ विश्वजिद् यद् रिपुञ्जय: ।बार्हद्रथाश्च भूपाला भाव्या: साहस्रवत्सरम् ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sunīthaḥ satyajid athaviśvajid yad ripuñjayaḥbārhadrathāś ca bhūpālābhāvyāḥ sāhasra-vatsaram
verses.meaning: From Subala will come Sunītha; from Sunītha, Satyajit; from Satyajit, Viśvajit; and from Viśvajit, Ripuñjaya. All of these personalities will belong to the dynasty of Bṛhadratha, which will rule the world for one thousand years.
verses.wordMeanings: sunīthaḥ; satyajit; atha; viśvajit; yat; ripuñjayaḥ; bārhadrathāḥ; ca; bhūpālāḥ; bhāvyāḥ; sāhasra; vatsaram
verses.purport: This is the history of a monarchy that began with Jarāsandha and continues for one thousand years as the above-mentioned kings appear on the surface of the globe.
